$comment: D-MIR-VERIFIER-UNBOUNDED-OPERAND-SENTINEL (c70, 2026-06-30): a `switch` with more than 255 cases. c69 cleared sqlite's MirFunctionRebuilder crash, so sqlite3.c compiled end-to-end and hit I_VerifierFailure 'opcode switch operand count 349 outside [1, 255]' — the c60 switch-flatten produces ONE MIR Switch opcode whose operands are [discriminant, caseValue*N]; sqlite has a ~348-case switch (349 operands). The MIR Switch opcode is VARIADIC (opcodeInfo maxOperands == kMirUnboundedOperands == 0xFF), and the builder (mir.cpp addInst) correctly exempts that sentinel from the upper-bound check, so it accepts >255 operands. But the verifier (src/mir/mir_verifier.cpp checkStructuralInvariants) did a plain `operands.size() > info.maxOperands`, reading the 0xFF sentinel as a literal max of 255 -> it rejected the Switch the builder had already built. Fix: the verifier mirrors the builder's exemption (`maxOperands != kMirUnboundedOperands && size > maxOperands`), so a variadic-operand opcode (Switch, IndirectBr) is not upper-bounded. This corpus's switch has 300 cases => 301 operands (>255): classify(k)=1000+k for k in [0,299], else 7 (default). Verified: classify(0/150/299)=1000/1150/1299, classify(300/-5)=7 -> 42. RED-ON-DISABLE: revert the verifier exemption -> I_VerifierFailure 'operand count 301 outside [1, 255]' (a compile failure, not 42). gcc/clang compile it and exit 42.
